steps.title: Understand what makes a solution
steps.content: A point $(x, y)$ satisfies the system if it makes BOTH inequalities true simultaneously. We'll test each choice by substituting its coordinates into both inequalities.

steps.title: Test each choice by substitution
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing Choice A — $(0, -14)$ $(0, -14)$:
First inequality: $-14 \le 0 + 3 = 3$ ✓
Second inequality: $-14 \ge -3(0) - 7 = -7$? No, $-14 \lt -7$ ✗
Fails.

Testing Choice B — $(0, 14)$ $(0, 14)$:
First inequality: $14 \le 0 + 3 = 3$? No, $14 \gt 3$ ✗
Fails immediately.

Testing Choice C — $(-14, 0)$ $(-14, 0)$:
First inequality: $0 \le -14 + 3 = -11$? No, $0 \gt -11$ ✗
Fails immediately.

Testing Choice D — $(14, 0)$ $(14, 0)$:
First inequality: $0 \le 14 + 3 = 17$ ✓
Second inequality: $0 \ge -3(14) - 7 = -49$ ✓
Both inequalities are satisfied. That matches Choice D — $(14, 0)$.
